search_summary.search_strategy: Direct repository analysis via git clone, examined sidebar navigation definitions, component implementations, menu systems, and detail views

repository_analysis.name: FreeLens
repository_analysis.description: Free and open-source Kubernetes IDE, community fork of Open Lens v5
strengths: Comprehensive Kubernetes API coverage (all standard resources)
Modular component structure with clear separation of concerns
Intelligent state-aware context menus (e.g., delete modes based on pod phase)
Full terminal integration with shell exec, attach, and node access
Production-ready Helm chart management (install, upgrade, rollback)
Multi-cluster support with kubeconfig context switching
Monaco editor integration for YAML editing
weaknesses: No incident management or RCA capabilities
No PII detection or data privacy features
No built-in audit logging beyond Kubernetes audit logs
use_cases: Helm chart deployment and lifecycle management

performance.scalability: Handles clusters with thousands of resources via watch API and store caching

implementation_patterns.usage: All services, stores, and components use injectable pattern with dedicated injection tokens
KubeObjectMenu dynamically generates actions based on resource state (deletionTimestamp, finalizers, phase)
examples: Pod delete modes: delete, force_delete, force_finalize
Port forward start/stop based on status
cons: Complex state logic in menu components
Requires careful testing of all state combinations

implementation_patterns.usage: Each Kubernetes resource has a dedicated MobX store with watch API integration
pros: Reactive UI updates via watch API
Easy to query and filter resources
cons: Potential stale data if watch disconnects

implementation_patterns.usage: Reusable tabbed panel for logs, terminal, editors with separate tab state management
pros: Parallel workflows (view logs while editing YAML)
cons: Limited screen real estate on smaller displays

implementation_patterns.usage: Right-side drawer displays resource details with expandable sections
examples: PodDetails with containers, volumes, secrets, conditions
DeploymentDetails with strategy, replicas, pod template
pros: Rich detail view without cluttering list
cons: Drawer can be narrow on smaller screens

best_practices.practice: Intelligent action availability based on resource state
best_practices.rationale: Prevents user errors by only showing actions that are valid for the current resource state (e.g., force delete only for Running/Pending pods)
best_practices.implementation: Check deletionTimestamp, finalizers, phase, and container status before rendering menu items
examples: Node cordon/uncordon toggle based on spec.unschedulable

best_practices.rationale: All delete, drain, restart, rollback actions require user confirmation with resource name displayed
best_practices.implementation: withConfirmation HOC wraps onClick handlers with a confirmation dialog

best_practices.rationale: Multi-container pods require selecting which container to shell into, view logs from, or attach to
best_practices.implementation: PodMenuItem component renders a submenu with container selection when multiple containers exist
examples: Logs menu with init, main, and ephemeral container options

best_practices.practice: Monaco editor for YAML editing with validation
best_practices.rationale: Provides syntax highlighting, autocomplete, and client-side validation before applying changes
best_practices.implementation: Monaco editor component with Kubernetes YAML schemas

best_practices.rationale: Modular sidebar structure allows extensions to inject custom navigation items
best_practices.implementation: Each resource registers a sidebar item via sidebarItemInjectionToken with parentId and orderNumber

common_pitfalls.impact: Common Rust anti-pattern; not applicable to FreeLens (TypeScript/JavaScript)
common_pitfalls.solution: N/A for FreeLens, but relevant for TFTSR Rust backend

common_pitfalls.pitfall: Not re-reading resource state before executing actions
common_pitfalls.impact: Stale state from MobX store can lead to invalid operations (e.g., force delete on already-terminated pod)
common_pitfalls.solution: KubeObjectMenu fetches latest object from store before action: `const latestObject = store?.getByPath(object.selfLink) || object;`

common_pitfalls.pitfall: Showing force delete for terminal pod phases
common_pitfalls.impact: Force delete has no effect on Succeeded, Failed, or Unknown pods; confuses users
common_pitfalls.solution: Skip force delete mode for terminal phases: `if (podPhase === PodStatusPhase.SUCCEEDED || podPhase === PodStatusPhase.FAILED || podPhase === 'Unknown') return ['delete'];`

common_pitfalls.impact: Resource stores become stale if watch API disconnects; UI shows outdated data
common_pitfalls.solution: Implement reconnection logic and periodic full refreshes
examples: Not explicitly visible in code examined, likely handled by Kubernetes client library

recommended_libraries.name: @ogre-tools/injectable
recommended_libraries.why_recommended: Enables modular architecture with testable components and clear dependency graphs. FreeLens uses this extensively for all services and UI components.

recommended_libraries.name: MobX
recommended_libraries.why_recommended: Simplifies reactive UI updates when Kubernetes watch API fires events. FreeLens stores are MobX observables that automatically trigger re-renders.

recommended_libraries.name: Monaco Editor
recommended_libraries.why_recommended: Industry-standard editor (powers VS Code), provides excellent YAML editing experience with schemas and validation.

recommended_libraries.name: Electron
recommended_libraries.why_recommended: Used by FreeLens for cross-platform desktop app. For TFTSR, Tauri is a better fit (Rust backend, smaller binaries).

architecture_suggestions.suggestion: Separate Kubernetes API layer from UI components
architecture_suggestions.context: When building K8s management features in TFTSR
benefits: Testable API logic without UI dependencies
Reusable API clients across different views

architecture_suggestions.suggestion: Use state-aware context menus instead of static action lists
architecture_suggestions.context: For pod, deployment, and other resource actions
benefits: Prevents invalid operations (e.g., force delete on terminated pod)
Cleaner UX with only applicable actions shown
Reduces need for error handling after action click

architecture_suggestions.suggestion: Implement dock/panel system for logs, terminal, editors
architecture_suggestions.context: For parallel workflows (view logs while editing YAML)
trade_offs: Increased memory usage for multiple tabs

architecture_suggestions.suggestion: Use injectable pattern for sidebar navigation
benefits: Easy to add new resource types without modifying core
Extensions can inject custom menu items
trade_offs: More setup code for simple static menus

security_recommendations.recommendation: Never log sensitive data from Kubernetes resources
security_recommendations.implementation: Implement PII detection before logging ConfigMaps, Secrets, or pod env vars. FreeLens does not have built-in PII detection; TFTSR should add this.

security_recommendations.recommendation: Audit all kubectl exec, apply, delete commands
security_recommendations.implementation: Log every shell command, YAML apply, and resource deletion with user, timestamp, and resource details. FreeLens does not have built-in audit logging; TFTSR should add this.

security_recommendations.recommendation: Validate YAML before applying to cluster
security_recommendations.implementation: Use client-side validation (JSON schema) and dry-run before applying changes. FreeLens uses Monaco editor with YAML schemas.
Store kubeconfig with AES-256 encryption, decrypt only when needed for API calls. FreeLens stores kubeconfig in plain text (security gap).

metrics.description: CPU and memory usage visualization (requires metrics-server)

features: Filter all views to selected namespace(s)

key_players.name: Freelens Authors
key_players.impact: Core development team maintaining fork after Lens Desktop went proprietary

key_players.name: OpenLens Authors
key_players.impact: Original open-source Lens project (2022), now archived

code_examples.explanation: Intelligent delete mode selection prevents showing force delete for terminal pod phases where it would have no effect

code_examples.purpose: Pod shell execution with container selection
code_examples.explanation: Pod shell menu constructs kubectl exec command with auto-detection of best shell (bash, ash, sh) for Linux or PowerShell for Windows nodes
Sidebar items use injectable pattern with parentId and orderNumber for hierarchical navigation tree
